title: Translation Validation
description: Output of Stage 3.5 (Validate Translation). Records the result of verifying that the generated scorer plugin faithfully implements the EVOLVE-BLOCK logic. Consumed by Stage 4 prerequisites and Stage 6 evidence generation. verdict 'pass' means zero deviations; 'pass_with_known_gaps' means only minor/intentional deviations remain; 'fail' means critical or important deviations remain after retries.

evolve_block_hash.description: SHA-256 of the EVOLVE-BLOCK source content, verified at the start of Stage 3.5 to guard against source drift.

scorer_file.description: Relative path to the generated scorer file, taken from stage3_output.json scorer_file field.

signal_checks.description: One entry per signal in algorithm_summary.json. Mechanical check that the production metric name appears in the scorer source.

present_in_scorer.description: True if prod_name appears in the scorer source file.

normalization_correct.description: True if the required normalization (e.g., divide_prod_by_100) is present in the scorer. Null (field absent or JSON null) if no normalization is required for this signal.

notes.description: Optional notes about the signal check result.

constant_checks.description: One entry per evolved numeric constant extracted from the EVOLVE-BLOCK. Mechanical check that each constant appears in the scorer source.

evolve_block_context.description: Code snippet or description of where the constant appears in the EVOLVE-BLOCK (e.g., 'decay factor in 1/(1+0.6*delta)').

present_in_scorer.description: True if the constant (or an equivalent form) appears in the scorer source.

scorer_context.description: Code snippet or description of where the constant appears in the scorer, if found.

logic_trace_checks.description: One entry per EVOLVE-BLOCK logic step checked by the LLM logic trace. check_id follows the pattern 'logic_3X_description' matching the validate-translation.md step identifiers.

check_id.description: Identifier matching the check table in validate-translation.md (e.g., 'logic_3d_adaptive_decay').

status.description: pass = equivalent logic found; deviation = logic differs or missing; intentional_gap = design decision not to translate.

severity.description: Required when status is 'deviation' or 'intentional_gap'. critical = changes ranking behavior; important = changes score magnitudes; minor = equivalent reordering or style; intentional = documented design decision.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what was found for this check.

evolve_block_lines.description: Line range in the EVOLVE-BLOCK source file (e.g., '203-223').

scorer_lines.description: Line range in the generated scorer file where the corresponding logic appears (e.g., '162-166'), or null/absent if the logic is missing.

known_gaps.description: Intentional translation gaps — EVOLVE-BLOCK elements deliberately not translated to production, with rationale.

description.description: What EVOLVE-BLOCK behavior is not translated.

rationale.description: Why this is intentional (e.g., 'Framework performs argmax; scorer returns per-endpoint scores').

deviations.description: Unintentional deviations found. Empty on pass or pass_with_known_gaps. Populated when verdict is fail (unfixed) or when fixed deviations are recorded for traceability.

check_id.description: Matches the check_id from logic_trace_checks or 'signal_<name>' / 'constant_<value>' for mechanical checks.

severity.description: critical = ranking behavior changed; important = score magnitudes changed; minor = equivalent reordering.

fixed.description: True if this deviation was corrected during Stage 3.5 fix attempts.

fix_description.description: What fix was applied, if fixed is true.

fix_attempts.description: Retry counters for fix attempts made during Stage 3.5.

retries_translation.description: Number of translation fix retries attempted (limit 3, halt after 2 retries done).

retries_total.description: Total fix retries across all check categories (limit 4, halt after 3 retries done).

verdict.description: pass = zero deviations of any severity. pass_with_known_gaps = only minor/intentional deviations; critical and important all absent or fixed. fail = critical or important deviations remain after retries exhausted.
